title: Great Pyramid of Giza
description: Built as a tomb for Pharaoh Khufu; the oldest of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

title: Stonehenge
description: Prehistoric megalithic structure on Salisbury Plain, England, built in several phases.

title: Indus Valley Civilization
description: Bronze Age civilization along the Indus River; one of the world's earliest urban cultures.

title: Code of Hammurabi
description: One of the earliest complete written legal codes, issued by Babylonian king Hammurabi.

title: Trojan War
description: Legendary conflict between Greeks and the city of Troy, recounted in Homer's Iliad.

title: Founding of Rome
description: Traditional date of Rome's founding by Romulus, established as a kingdom on the Tiber River.

title: Siddhartha Gautama (Buddha)
description: Founder of Buddhism, born in Lumbini (modern Nepal), who taught the path to enlightenment.

title: Confucius
description: Chinese philosopher whose teachings on ethics and social harmony shaped East Asian civilization.

title: Battle of Marathon
description: Athenian forces repelled a Persian invasion, securing Greek independence and democracy.

title: Alexander the Great
description: Macedonian king who built one of history's largest empires, from Greece to northwestern India.

title: Great Wall of China
description: Fortification system across northern China; major construction began under Emperor Qin Shi Huang.

title: Julius Caesar
description: Roman general and statesman whose military conquests and political reforms transformed Rome.

title: Birth of Jesus
description: Approximate birth and death years of Jesus of Nazareth, central figure of Christianity.

title: Fall of Western Roman Empire
description: Romulus Augustulus, the last Roman emperor of the West, was deposed by Odoacer.

title: Muhammad
description: Prophet and founder of Islam, born in Mecca; his teachings became the Quran.

title: Viking Age
description: Norse seafarers from Scandinavia explored, traded, and raided across Europe and beyond.

title: Battle of Hastings
description: William the Conqueror defeated King Harold II, fundamentally changing English culture and language.

title: Magna Carta
description: English charter limiting royal power and establishing rule of law; signed at Runnymede.

title: Black Death
description: Bubonic plague pandemic killed roughly one-third of Europe's population within six years.

title: Gutenberg Printing Press
description: Johannes Gutenberg's movable-type printing press revolutionized information dissemination in Europe.

title: Fall of Constantinople
description: Ottoman forces under Mehmed II captured Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

title: Columbus Reaches Americas
description: Christopher Columbus landed in the Bahamas, initiating sustained contact between Europe and the Americas.

title: Leonardo da Vinci
description: Italian Renaissance polymath: painter of the Mona Lisa, anatomist, engineer, and inventor.

title: Magellan's Circumnavigation
description: Ferdinand Magellan's expedition became the first to circumnavigate the globe, departing from Seville.

title: Nicolaus Copernicus
description: Polish astronomer who proposed the heliocentric model of the solar system.

title: William Shakespeare
description: English playwright and poet; widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language.

title: Galileo Galilei
description: Italian physicist and astronomer who championed heliocentrism and pioneered scientific experimentation.

title: Isaac Newton
description: English mathematician who formulated laws of motion and universal gravitation.

title: American Revolution
description: Colonial uprising against British rule that established the United States of America.

title: French Revolution
description: Period of radical political transformation in France that ended the monarchy and spread republican ideals.

title: Industrial Revolution
description: Shift from agrarian to industrial economy in Britain, transforming manufacturing and society globally.

title: Napoleonic Empire
description: Napoleon Bonaparte's rule reshaped Europe through military conquest and legal-political reforms.

title: Charles Darwin
description: English naturalist who proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection.

title: Abolition of Slavery (USA)
description: The Thirteenth Amendment abolished slavery in the United States following the Civil War.

title: Telephone Invented
description: Alexander Graham Bell transmitted the first intelligible voice message by telephone in Boston.

title: First Powered Flight
description: Orville and Wilbur Wright achieved the first sustained powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk, NC.

title: World War I
description: Global conflict centered in Europe killing ~20 million; reshaped borders and ended four empires.

title: Russian Revolution
description: Bolshevik revolution led by Lenin overthrew the tsarist government, creating the Soviet Union.

title: World War II
description: Global conflict involving most of the world's nations; deadliest in history (~70–85 million dead).

title: Indian Independence
description: India gained independence from British rule on August 15, 1947, becoming the world's largest democracy.

title: Apollo 11 Moon Landing
description: NASA astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon.

title: ARPANET / Internet Origins
description: The first message was sent over ARPANET in Los Angeles, the precursor to the modern internet.

title: Fall of Berlin Wall
description: The Berlin Wall fell, symbolizing the end of the Cold War and leading to German reunification.
